operating_state: Total revenue increased 2% to $13.9 billion driven by strength in premium products and long-haul international travel despite 4% capacity expansion. Passenger revenue grew $349 million on premium product demand and loyalty travel awards, while total adjusted revenue increased 3.3% excluding refinery sales. Operating income declined $45 million to $569 million as revenue growth was offset by higher operating expenses.
cost_structure: Total operating expenses increased 3% primarily from capacity expansion costs, higher employee wages, and increased landing fees, partially offset by lower aircraft fuel costs. Total cost per available seat mile decreased 2% while non-fuel unit costs increased 2.6%. Operating margin compressed as expense growth outpaced revenue gains despite fuel cost relief.
material_events: Judith McKenna was elected to the Board of Directors effective February 7, 2025. The company generated $2.4 billion in operating cash flow and $1.3 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. Delta made $531 million in debt and finance lease repayments while maintaining $6.8 billion in total liquidity.
macro_exposures: Management revised March quarter guidance downward citing reduced consumer and corporate confidence from increased macro uncertainty driving softness in domestic demand. The company lowered total revenue growth expectations from 7-9% to 3-4% and operating margin guidance from 6-8% to 4-5%.
forward_outlook: Management expects March quarter total revenue growth of 3-4% year-over-year with operating margin of 4-5% and earnings per share of $0.30-$0.50. Premium, international and loyalty revenue growth trends remain consistent with expectations, demonstrating resilience in the diversified revenue base despite domestic demand softness.
uncertainty_profile: No material changes were reported to the risk factors described in the Form 10-K. Standard forward-looking statement disclosures reference risks including fuel cost volatility, technology disruptions, labor issues, environmental regulations, and geopolitical conflicts without indicating material escalation in any specific risk category.
